revision.comment: [[直毘神]] へのリダイレクトを解除しました
revision.text:
[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
また、神直日神の読みとして「かむなおひのかみ,かむなほひのかみ,かみなおひのかみ,かみなほひのかみ,かんなおひのかみ,かんなほひのかみ」などがある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
折口信夫はナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[瀬織津姫]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむなおひのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:邪神・悪神]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
また、神直日神の読みとして「かむなおひのかみ,かむなほひのかみ,かみなおひのかみ,かみなほひのかみ,かんなおひのかみ,かんなほひのかみ」などがある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
折口信夫による考証 ・ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 ・元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 ・平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[瀬織津姫]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむなおひのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:邪神・悪神]]

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
また、神直日神の読みとして「かむなおひのかみ,かむなほひのかみ,かみなおひのかみ,かみなほひのかみ,かんなおひのかみ,かんなほひのかみ」などがある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
折口信夫によると、次のような考証がなされている。<ref name="U">[[折口信夫]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[中央公論新社]] 。</ref>。 * ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 * 元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 * 平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。

== 概要 == 早吸日女神社 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 西寒多神社 大分県大分市寒田 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 鵜戸神宮 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 綾戸國中神社 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 厳島神社 広島県廿日市市宮島町 石上神宮 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町

== 関連する神社 == *直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。

== 神直日神・大直日神 を祀る主な神社==
*警固神社 福岡県福岡市中央区天神 *櫻井神社 福岡県糸島市志摩桜井 *直日神社 福岡県直方市日吉町 * 早吸日女神社 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 * 西寒多神社 大分県大分市寒田 * 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 * 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 * 鵜戸神宮 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 * 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 * 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 * 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 * 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 * 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 * 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 * 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 * 綾戸國中神社 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 * 厳島神社 広島県廿日市市宮島町 * 石上神宮 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
折口信夫によると、次のような考証がなされている。<ref name="U">[[折口信夫]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[中央公論新社]] 。</ref>。 * ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 * 元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 * 平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。
== 考証 == 折口信夫によると、次のような考証がなされている。<ref name="U">[[折口信夫]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[中央公論新社]] 。</ref>。 * ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 * 元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 * 平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。

[[Image:Creation myths of Japan 5.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 穢れを払い、禍（まが）を直す神とされる。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
== 考証 == 折口信夫によると、次のような考証がなされている。<ref name="U">[[折口信夫]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[中央公論新社]] 。</ref>。 * ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 * 元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 * 平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。
== 神直日神・大直日神 を祀る主な神社==
*[[警固神社]] 福岡県福岡市中央区天神 *櫻井神社 福岡県糸島市志摩桜井 *直日神社 福岡県直方市日吉町 *[[早吸日女神社]] 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 *[[西寒多神社]] 大分県大分市寒田 * 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 * 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 * [[鵜戸神宮]] 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 * 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 * 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 * 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 * 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 * 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 * 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 * 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 * [[綾戸国中神社|綾戸國中神社]] 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 * [[厳島神社]] 広島県廿日市市宮島町 * [[石上神宮]] 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町 など
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[瀬織津姫]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむなおひのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:邪神・悪神]]

== 神直日神・大直日神 を祀る主な神社==
*[[警固神社]] 福岡県福岡市中央区天神 *[[桜井神社 (糸島市)|櫻井神社(糸島市)]] 福岡県糸島市志摩桜井 *直日神社 福岡県直方市日吉町 *[[早吸日女神社]] 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 *[[西寒多神社]] 大分県大分市寒田 * 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 * 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 * [[鵜戸神宮]] 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 * 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 * 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 * 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 * 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 * 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 * 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 * 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 * [[綾戸国中神社|綾戸國中神社]] 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 * [[厳島神社]] 広島県廿日市市宮島町 * [[石上神宮]] 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町 など

revision.comment: Created page with " # {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|ja|八十禍津日神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}, male deity Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born: # {{ill|Kamuna-obi|ja|神直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|ja|伊豆能売}}, female deity Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born; # {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神}}, gen..."
revision.text:
# {{ill|Yasomaga-Tsuhi|ja|八十禍津日神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōmaga-Tsuhi|ja|大禍津日神}}, male deity
Then, shaking off the curse, three gods were born:
# {{ill|Kamuna-obi|ja|神直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}, male deity # {{ill|Izunome (kami)|ja|伊豆能売}}, female deity
Then, when washing with water the lower parts of his body, two gods were born;
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Sokotsu-watatsumi]]'''|底津綿津見神}}, genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sokotsutsuno'o]]'''|底筒之男神}}, male deity
When washing the middle of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神}}, genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神}}, male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神}},<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神}}, male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神}} was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命}} was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命}} = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”}}, Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神}} from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86}}</ref>
==Notes== {{reflist|group="nb"}}
==References== {{reflist}}
===Bibliography=== *{{cite book|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|title=The Kojiki: Japanese Records of Ancient Matters|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=zVw392cJrJoC|access-date=9 February 2011|year=2008|publisher=Forgotten Books|isbn=978-1-60506-938-8}} *{{cite web |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071110023248/http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf |title=Génesis del mundo y aparición de los primeros dioses|trans-title=Genesis of the world and appearance of the first gods|archive-date=2007-11-10|language=es|format=PDF|url=http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf}}

The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. # {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Nakatsu-watatsumi]]'''|中津綿津見神]], genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Nakatsutsuno'o]]'''|中筒之男神]], male deity
Finally, washing the upper part of his body, two more gods were born:
# {{nihongo|'''[[Watatsumi|Uwatsu-watatsumi]]'''|上津綿津見神]],<ref group="nb">Chamberlain: Uhatsuwatatsumi</ref> genderless deity and spirit # {{nihongo|'''[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Uwatsutsuno'o]]'''|上筒之男神]], male deity
The trio of Sokotsu-watatsumi, Nakatsu-watatsumi and Uwatsu-watatsumi make up the group of deities called [[Watatsumi|Sanjin Watatsumi]], or the gods of water. The trio of Sokotsutsuno'o, Nakatsutsuno'o and Uhatsutsuno'o make up the [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] group of deities, gods of fishing and sea, to whom tribute is paid at [[Sumiyoshi Taisha]].<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
In the last step of the purification ceremony, Izanagi washed his left eye from which the female deity {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]'''|天照大御神]] was born; washed his right eye from which the genderless deity and spirit {{nihongo|'''[[Tsukuyomi|Tsukuyomi-no-mikoto]]'''|月読命]] was born; and when washing his nose from which the male deity {{nihongo|'''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Takehaya-susano'o-no-mikoto]]'''|建速須佐之男命]] = commonly known as Susano'o was born.<ref name="kojiki-p84-85"/>
With these three gods called {{nihongo|[[Mihashira-no-uzu-no-miko]]|三貴子|extra=”Three precious children”]], Izanagi ordered their investiture. Amaterasu received the mandate to govern [[Takamagahara]] and a necklace of jewels called {{nihongo|Mikuratanano-no-kami|御倉板挙之神]] from Izanagi. Tsukuyomi is mandated to govern over the Dominion of the Night, and Takehaya-susano'o (建速須佐之男命) = Susano'o is to rule the seas.<ref name="kojiki-p86">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|2008|p=86]]</ref>
==Notes== {{reflist|group="nb"]]
==References== {{reflist]]
===Bibliography=== *{{cite book|last=Chamberlain|first=Basil Hall|title=The Kojiki: Japanese Records of Ancient Matters|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=zVw392cJrJoC|access-date=9 February 2011|year=2008|publisher=Forgotten Books|isbn=978-1-60506-938-8]] *{{cite web |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071110023248/http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf |title=Génesis del mundo y aparición de los primeros dioses|trans-title=Genesis of the world and appearance of the first gods|archive-date=2007-11-10|language=es|format=PDF|url=http://www.clubmanga.net/files/cursos/historia/CH_Mitologia.pdf]]

revision.text: [[ja:神直日神]]

[[ja:神直日神]] [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification ritual (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (for compatible browsers)]]]
'''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] ''Dictionary of Eastern Deities,'' {{ill|Shinkigensha|ja|新紀元社}}, p. 349.</ref> This deity is regarded as one who purifies defilements and rectifies calamities (禍, ''maga''). In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the {{ill|birth of deities|ja|神産み}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izanome|ja|伊豆能売}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' {{ill|Chūōkōron-shinsha|ja|中央公論新社}}.</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izanome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * {{ill|Saikanda Shrine|ja|西寒多神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * {{ill|Udo Shrine|ja|鵜戸神宮}}, within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}

[[ja:神直日神]] [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification ritual (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (for compatible browsers)]]]
'''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] ''Dictionary of Eastern Deities,'' {{ill|Shinkigensha|ja|新紀元社}}, p. 349.</ref> This deity is regarded as one who purifies defilements and rectifies calamities (禍, ''maga''). In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the {{ill|birth of deities|ja|神産み}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izanome|ja|伊豆能売}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' {{ill|Chūōkōron-shinsha|ja|中央公論新社}}.</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izanome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * {{ill|Saikanda Shrine|ja|西寒多神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * {{ill|Udo Shrine|ja|鵜戸神宮}}, within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}

== Analysis == According to the scholar {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' {{ill|Chūōkōron-shinsha|ja|中央公論新社}}.</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izanome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * {{ill|Saikanda Shrine|ja|西寒多神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * {{ill|Udo Shrine|ja|鵜戸神宮}}, within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}

revision.text:
[[ja:神直日神]] [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification ritual (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (for compatible browsers)]]]
'''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] ''Dictionary of Eastern Deities,'' {{ill|Shinkigensha|ja|新紀元社}}, p. 349.</ref> This deity is regarded as one who purifies defilements and rectifies calamities (禍, ''maga''). In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the {{ill|birth of deities|ja|神産み}}, when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} returned from {{ill|Yomi|ja|黄泉}}, the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izanome|ja|伊豆能売}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar {{ill|Shinobu Orikuchi|ja|折口信夫}}, the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' {{ill|Chūōkōron-shinsha|ja|中央公論新社}}.</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izanome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * {{ill|Saikanda Shrine|ja|西寒多神社}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * {{ill|Udo Shrine|ja|鵜戸神宮}}, within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|ja|坐摩神社}}, within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}}

14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:神直日神 up to rev 99353895
revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|イザナギの禊ぎによって生まれた神々（『古事記』に基づく） [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg SVGで表示（対応ブラウザのみ）]]] '''神直日神'''（かむなおひのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]である<ref name="T">[[山北篤]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[新紀元社]] 349頁。</ref>。 穢れを払い、禍（まが）を直す神とされる。 [[古事記]]では神直毘神（かむなほびのかみ）と、[[日本書紀]]では神直日神（かむなおひのかみ）と記載される。その他、神直備神と記載されることもある。
== 概要 == [[神産み]]において[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]が[[黄泉]]から帰って来た際、黄泉の[[穢れ]]から[[禍津日神]]が生まれた。その禍津日神がもたらす禍（災厄）を直すために、[[直毘神]]の一柱として生まれた神が'''神直日神'''とされる。また、その後、二柱目の神として[[大直日神]]が生まれ、最後に[[伊豆能売]]が生まれたとされる。
== 考証 == 折口信夫によると、次のような考証がなされている。<ref name="U">[[折口信夫]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[中央公論新社]] 。</ref>。 * ナホビの神はマガツヒの神との対句として発生した表裏一体の神であるとしている。また、直毘神は穢れを祓う神事を行う際の祭主であり、伊豆能売は巫女であるとも考えられる。 * 元来なほるといふ語は、直日の神の「直」と関係のある語で、間違いのあった時に、匡正してくれる神が、直日の神とする。 * 平安朝に入ってからは、直日の神というのは、宴会の神、又は遊芸の神とされる。
== 神直日神・大直日神 を祀る主な神社==
*[[警固神社]] 福岡県福岡市中央区天神 *[[桜井神社 (糸島市)|櫻井神社(糸島市)]] 福岡県糸島市志摩桜井 *直日神社 福岡県直方市日吉町 *[[早吸日女神社]] 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 *[[西寒多神社]] 大分県大分市寒田 * 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 * 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 * [[鵜戸神宮]] 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 * 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 * 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 * 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 * 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 * 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 * 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 * 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 * [[綾戸国中神社|綾戸國中神社]] 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 * [[厳島神社]] 広島県廿日市市宮島町 * [[石上神宮]] 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町 など
== 出典 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[瀬織津姫]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かむなおひのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:邪神・悪神]]

*[[警固神社]] 福岡県福岡市中央区天神 *[[桜井神社 (糸島市)|櫻井神社(糸島市)]] 福岡県糸島市志摩桜井 *直日神社 福岡県直方市日吉町 *[[早吸日女神社]] 大分県大分市大字佐賀関 *[[西寒多神社]] 大分県大分市寒田 * 出羽神社 三神合祭殿 境内 大直日神社 山形県鶴岡市羽黒町 * 五泉八幡宮 新潟県五泉市宮町 * [[鵜戸神宮]] 境内 九柱神社 宮崎県日南市大字宮浦 * 伊蘇乃佐只神社 鳥取県八頭郡八頭町安井宿字宮ノ後 * 大原神社 境内 桜井神社 島根県邑智郡邑南町日貫 * 野間神社 神門 愛媛県今治市神宮字杉ノ下甲 * 神部神社 山梨県甲州市塩山上萩原 * 大縣神社 境内 解除社 愛知県犬山市宮山 * 坐摩神社 境内 相殿神社 大阪府大阪市中央区久太郎町 * 伊久刀神社 兵庫県豊岡市日高町赤崎字家ノ上 * [[綾戸国中神社|綾戸國中神社]] 京都府京都市南区久世上久世町 * [[厳島神社]] 広島県廿日市市宮島町 * [[石上神宮]] 境内 七座社 奈良県天理市布留町 など

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神直日神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86725467]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q86725467]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification ritual (based on the ''Kojiki'') [//upload.wikimedia.org/]]]
'''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] ''Dictionary of Eastern Deities,'' {{ill|Shinkigensha|ja|新紀元社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkigensha|qq=|1=Shinkigensha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503908}}, p. 349.</ref> This deity is regarded as one who purifies defilements and rectifies calamities (禍, ''maga''). In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|birth of deities]], when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|qq=|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|qq=|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

'''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] ''Dictionary of Eastern Deities,'' {{ill|Shinkigensha|ja|新紀元社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkigensha|qq=|1=Shinkigensha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503908}}, p. 349.</ref> This deity is regarded as one who purifies defilements and rectifies calamities (禍, ''maga''). In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|birth of deities]], when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|qq=|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuihime Shrine|qq=|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]] – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]], within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]], within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|qq=|1=Ayatokunaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607305}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.

To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the deity is referred to as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), while in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', it is written as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神). The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|birth of deities]], when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To rectify the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

Following this, a second deity, '''Ōnaobi no Kami''' (大直日神), was born, and finally {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}} was born.
== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuihime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]] – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]], within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]], within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|1=Ayatokunaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607305}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

[[ja:神直日神]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

== Analysis == According to the scholar [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], the following interpretations have been proposed:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], ''Collected Works of Shinobu Orikuchi (New Edition),'' [[:en:Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-shinsha]].</ref> * The ''Naobi'' deity was conceptualized as a counterpart to Magatsuhi no Kami, representing two sides of the same coin. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.

[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuihime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]] – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]], within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]], within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|1=Ayatokunaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607305}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

[[ja:神直日神]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

The name also appears as '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直備神) in some texts.
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|birth of deities]], when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], the defilements of the underworld gave rise to the deity {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.

The {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}} is seen as the priest who performs purification rituals, while Izunome is considered a shamaness (miko). * The verb ''naoru'' (to correct or heal) is related to the "直" (nao) in ''Naobi no Kami,'' symbolizing a deity that corrects mistakes or restores balance. * During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], Naobi no Kami was revered as a deity of banquets and entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}} – Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}} – Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine – Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuihime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuihime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}} – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]] – Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * Dewasanzan Shrine, Sanshin Gōsai-den – Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine – Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]], within the grounds of Kyūhashira Shrine – Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isonosaji Shrine – Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine, within the grounds of Sakurai Shrine – Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine – Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kambe Shrine – Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine, within the grounds of Kaijo Shrine – Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Ikasuri Shrine]], within the grounds of Aiden Shrine – Chūō Ward, Osaka City * Ikuto Shrine – Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Kuninaka Shrine|1=Ayatokunaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11607305}} – Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] – Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], within the grounds of Shichiza Shrine – Tenri City, Nara Prefecture

[[ja:神直日神]]
[[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== [[Image:Descendants of izanagi alone.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities born from Izanagi's purification (based on the Kojiki) [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Creation_myths_of_Japan_5.svg View in SVG (supported browsers only)]]]] '''Kamunaobi no Kami''' (神直日神, also spelled かむなおひのかみ) is a deity in [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]].<ref name="T">[[Atsushi Yamakita]] 『東洋神名事典』 [[:en:Gakken|Gakken]] p. 349.</ref> This deity is said to purify defilements and rectify {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi|ja|禍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi|qq=}} (misfortunes or evils). In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the deity is referred to as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直毘神), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] refers to the deity as Kamunaohi no Kami (神直日神). Other texts also spell the name as Kamunaobi no Kami (神直備神).
== Overview == During the [[:en:Kamiumi|Birth of the Gods]], {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} returned from [[:en:Yomi|Yomi]], where defilements from Yomi gave rise to {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}}. To counteract the calamities caused by Magatsuhi no Kami, Kamunaobi no Kami was born as one of the {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Ōnaobi no Kami|lt=Naobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naobi no Kami|qq=}}. Subsequently, another deity, {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}, was born, followed by {{ill|Izunome|ja|伊豆能売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunome|qq=}}.
== Historical Interpretations == According to [[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]], Kamunaobi no Kami has the following attributes:<ref name="U">[[:en:Shinobu Orikuchi|Shinobu Orikuchi]] 『折口信夫全集 新版』 [[:en:Chūōkōron-sha|Chūōkōron-sha]].</ref> * Naobi no Kami is considered a paired and interconnected deity with Magatsuhi no Kami, embodying their dual aspects. Additionally, Kamunaobi no Kami functions as the officiant deity in purification rituals, while Izunome is considered the miko (shrine maiden). * The term "naoru" (to rectify or correct) is linguistically related to the "Nao" (直) in Kamunaobi no Kami, implying a deity invoked to rectify missteps or wrongdoings. * By the Heian period, Kamunaobi no Kami was associated with being a deity of feasts or entertainment.
== Major Shrines Dedicated to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami == * {{ill|Kego Shrine|ja|警固神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine|qq=}}, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|ja|桜井神社 (糸島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Shrine (Itoshima)|1=Sakurai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118847}}, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture * Naobi Shrine, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture * {{ill|Hayasuhime Shrine|ja|早吸日女神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasuhime Shrine|1=Hayasuihime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511196}}, Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Sasamuta Shrine|Sasamuta Shrine]], Ōita City, Ōita Prefecture * [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] (Ōnaobi Shrine on the grounds), Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture * Gosen Hachiman Shrine, Gosen City, Niigata Prefecture * [[:en:Udo Jingū|Udo Shrine]] (Kyuhashira Shrine on the grounds), Nichinan City, Miyazaki Prefecture * Isono Sata Shrine, Yazu District, Tottori Prefecture * Ōhara Shrine (Sakurai Shrine on the grounds), Ōnan Town, Shimane Prefecture * Noma Shrine, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture * Kanbe Shrine, Kōshū City, Yamanashi Prefecture * Ōagata Shrine (Kaijo Shrine on the grounds), Inuyama City, Aichi Prefecture * [[:en:Ikasuri Shrine|Zama Shrine]] (Aiden Shrine on the grounds), Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture * Ikuta Shrine, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture * {{ill|Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayatokunaka Shrine|qq=}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] (Nanaza Shrine on the grounds), Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
